comment: Canonical Adjustments Register — every override / adjustment made to raw source data, consolidated from 6 underlying config files. Built by _etl_scripts/build_adjustments_register.mjs. A QoE reviewer should be able to answer "give me every adjustment you made to the raw data" from this single file. Drill into the source_file column to see the underlying config.

adjustments.reason: Flagged as an annual payer — lump-sum charges ≥ $3,000 are amortized across coverage months (default 12, override-capable). Trailing-18-month amortized contribution to MRR: $55,929.54 across 27 months.

Flagged as an annual payer — lump-sum charges ≥ $3,000 are amortized across coverage months (default 12, override-capable). Trailing-18-month amortized contribution to MRR: $24,170.67 across 23 months.
B&B Door $32,002.50 paid 2026-05-27 covers Mar 2026 through May 2027 — 3 retroactive months (Mar/Apr/May 2026) plus 12 forward (Jun 2026 → May 2027).
Card failure on 2026-05. Customer is still active; payment expected once card is updated. Treat as stable in the May waterfall.
Tiered / usage-based billing. No qualifying usage in May 2026 so no fee was charged, but customer is still active. Treat as stable in the May waterfall.
Customer still active — May charge had not posted at refresh time. (Note: "Door Works" customer record 504 is a duplicate of Drawer Works 505 — the latter is the real account with the subscription.)

Reclassified from subscription → services: Allmoxy Migration Project Invoice 5880 — Panhandle's one-off services project invoice. Two source-data issues to undo: (1) Stripe Sync's auto-classifier types it 'subscription' because the description doesn't contain 'services' (formula keyword match); (2) the latest Stripe Sync upload assigns it to allmoxy_customer_id 2018 (Stolbek) — sharing Stolbek's stripe_subscription_id sub_1T46pXHkM2QhTtxhSjowP9Pa — even though QuickBooks (and Beau, repeatedly) attribute the charge to Panhandle. Override moves the transaction back to Panhandle id 38 AND retypes it as services. Without this fix, Stolbek shows incorrectly as Contracted -$7,568 on Current Month (cluster matcher groups all of Stolbek's same-sub_id charges together). (transaction also moved from source customer Stolbek #2018)

Reclassified from subscription → subscription: Invoice 5D66E5C-0014 — M2M components' May 2026 subscription charge. Stripe Sync attached this charge to Adentra (aid 2032) because at Stripe it was recorded against sub_1SfpdpHkM2QhTtxhvL2gCCn3 (Adentra's subscription). But all the evidence — invoice prefix `5D66E5C-` matches M2M's series (sequential between -0013 Apr and -0015 Jun), amount $1,343 matches M2M's exact MRR, date 5/19 fits M2M's 19th-of-month cadence — confirms this belongs on M2M. Adentra's normal pattern is $200/mo on the 18th (invoice prefix `RUECNDQP-`); the $1,343 was the only outlier on their ledger. Without this fix, M2M is missing a May payment and Adentra shows a false contraction (May $1,343 → June $200). (transaction also moved from source customer Adentra #2032)

Reclassified from subscription → subscription: Reallocation of $1,698 catch-up payment received 2026-05-05 — Stripe charge description was 'March & April past due payments.' covering two failed invoices: 5952CF73-0010 (Mar 1, $849, failed) and 5952CF73-0011 (Apr 1, $849, failed). Without this reallocation, monthly_history reports $0 for Mar 2026, $0 for Apr 2026, and $1,698 for May 2026 — a misleading triple swing. With it, monthly_history reports $849/$849/$0 across Mar/Apr/May which matches the BILLING INTENT (accrual basis). QB RECONCILIATION: transactions[] is untouched — Stripe still shows the single $1,698 receipt on 5/5/2026 (cash basis), matching what QB's deposit/payment record will show. Difference is timing only: cash basis Mar+Apr = $0+$0 / May = $1,698; accrual basis Mar+Apr = $849+$849 / May = $0. Both sum to $1,698 across the three months.

Injected 1 Stripe customer ID(s): cus_UNWDyyeMOkW4BO. Formations paid $750 on 2026-04-30 (Stripe sub) but was missing from allmoxy_core_customer's stripe_customer_id_* fields, which made the charge orphan in the MRR by Month tab as a phantom $750 line in row 6.

Injected 1 Stripe customer ID(s): cus_ULd18BXCrI7KKB. Windsor Place paid $250 on 2026-04-16 (Stripe sub) but was missing from allmoxy_core_customer's stripe_customer_id_* fields. (Note: the ID contains a capital I, not lowercase l, in 'CrI7KKB' — confirmed against Stripe Sync raw data.)

Off-Stripe check (subscription): Annual subscription renewal — paid by mailed check (Dec 2025). Off-Stripe payment captured via synthetic_transactions override. Customer renewed annual subscription via mailed paper check in December 2025; payment never flowed through Stripe so it's invisible to our raw transaction ingest. Without this override, their amortization window (Nov 2024 lump-sum → Oct 2025 coverage) ends and they appear churned. With this override, the $12,474 amortizes as ~$1,039.50/mo over Dec 2025 → Nov 2026.

Forced status='active' (sub_instance_of_parent of #323 Architectural Woods Inc). Tree Products (treeproductsawi.allmoxy.com) is a secondary Allmoxy instance billed under the AWI parent (#323) $199/mo subscription, not a standalone payer. Their own Stripe activity stopped Jan 2024 when the billing consolidated under the parent; instance remains live as part of the parent agreement. Counting them as churned would mis-state retention; counting their MRR as $100/mo would double-count. Resolution: status=active, MRR=$0 (parent already carries the dollars). Evidence: HubSpot company 6088910932 (Architectural Woods LP) is shared by both #323 and #225; user-confirmed (2026-06-12) the Tree Products instance is active under the parent's subscription.
adjustments.evidence: HubSpot company 6088910932 (Architectural Woods LP) is shared by both #323 and #225; user-confirmed (2026-06-12) the Tree Products instance is active under the parent's subscription.

adjustments.reason: Forced status='active' (duplicate_of of #318 Thomas Creek). Ruck Cabinet Doors (#315) is a duplicate / pre-rebrand record for the same business that operates today as Thomas Creek (#318). The Ruck record has $0 lifetime, 0 transactions, no HubSpot ID — a phantom shell. Thomas Creek (#318) carries the full $134K lifetime and $2,610/mo active MRR. Without this override, Ruck appears as a $0 churn (false signal); with it, both records resolve cleanly to the same active customer. Evidence: User-confirmed (2026-06-12) Ruck Cabinet Doors is the same business as Thomas Creek. Thomas Creek (#318) Stripe transactions span 99 charges over multi-year history; Ruck (#315) has 0 transactions and no HubSpot company link — consistent with a renamed Allmoxy account.
adjustments.evidence: User-confirmed (2026-06-12) Ruck Cabinet Doors is the same business as Thomas Creek. Thomas Creek (#318) Stripe transactions span 99 charges over multi-year history; Ruck (#315) has 0 transactions and no HubSpot company link — consistent with a renamed Allmoxy account.

adjustments.reason: Forced status='churned' (duplicate_of of #322 Ultrashelf). Ultra Craftsmanship (#428) is the same business as Ultrashelf (#322) — a pre-rebrand record. Both have payment history (#428 paid through Jun 2023 with $31K lifetime; #322 took over and paid through Oct 2023 with $3.25K lifetime). Without dedup, this single business inflates the churned-logo count by 1. With this override, Ultra Craftsmanship mirrors Ultrashelf's churned status, MRR is zeroed (no double-count of historical churn dollars), and excluded_from_logo_count keeps it out of the logo waterfall as a separate entity. Evidence: User-confirmed (2026-06-12) Ultra Craftsmanship is the same business as Ultrashelf. Timing pattern (Ultra Craftsmanship Stripe activity ends Jun 2023; Ultrashelf activity continues through Oct 2023) is consistent with a mid-2023 rebrand and Allmoxy account rename. #428 has no HubSpot link; #322 maps to HubSpot company 3414592775.
adjustments.evidence: User-confirmed (2026-06-12) Ultra Craftsmanship is the same business as Ultrashelf. Timing pattern (Ultra Craftsmanship Stripe activity ends Jun 2023; Ultrashelf activity continues through Oct 2023) is consistent with a mid-2023 rebrand and Allmoxy account rename. #428 has no HubSpot link; #322 maps to HubSpot company 3414592775.

adjustments.reason: Forced status='active' (duplicate_of). Massachusetts Design Corp. (#468) rebranded to Julian Woods in 2025. HubSpot has them on a single Company record now labeled 'Julian Woods(formerly Massachusetts Design)' (HubSpot company ID 46167759542, originally 8474228273 — remapped on company merge). Same primary contact (Will Gascoigne); active CSM relationship — 64-minute meeting Dec 19 2025, $750 deal closed Apr 2026. Our Allmoxy customer record #468 still carries the old 'Massachusetts Design Corp.' name and shows as churned because the rebrand didn't update our records. No separate Julian Woods record exists in customer_profiles yet — when one is created, link it as the canonical via parent_allmoxy_customer_id. Until then, marking #468 as duplicate_of (orphaned canonical) so it falls out of churn counts. Evidence: HubSpot company 46167759542 (Julian Woods, formerly Massachusetts Design) — notes from 2025-12-19 (production order customization meeting), 2025-09-11 (Will Gascoigne meeting), 2025-09-02 (William Gascoigne meeting). Customer is actively engaging Allmoxy CSM team into late 2025 / early 2026.
adjustments.evidence: HubSpot company 46167759542 (Julian Woods, formerly Massachusetts Design) — notes from 2025-12-19 (production order customization meeting), 2025-09-11 (Will Gascoigne meeting), 2025-09-02 (William Gascoigne meeting). Customer is actively engaging Allmoxy CSM team into late 2025 / early 2026.

adjustments.reason: Forced status='churned' (duplicate_of of #457 Modern Fronts DBA - Wildwood Cabinets Incorporated). Wildwood Cabinets, Inc. (#308) is the same business as Modern Fronts DBA - Wildwood Cabinets Incorporated (#457) — a pre-rebrand record. #308 paid through Feb 2022 (lifetime $7K, 21 txns) before the rebrand; #457 took over with the new DBA name and is currently active (lifetime $21K, 122 txns, $419/mo MRR). Without dedup, this single business would show up as both a 2022 churn AND an active customer. With this override, #308 mirrors #457's active status, MRR is zeroed, and excluded_from_logo_count keeps it out of the logo waterfall. Evidence: User-confirmed (2026-06-12) — HubSpot custom-object record 2-39181518/22267106658 links Modern Fronts and Wildwood Cabinets. #308 HubSpot company 2551073029 (Wildwood Cabinets, Inc.); #457 HubSpot company 37315206430 (Modern Fronts DBA - Wildwood Cabinets Incorporated). Account naming + payment-timeline transition consistent with a 2022 rebrand.
adjustments.evidence: User-confirmed (2026-06-12) — HubSpot custom-object record 2-39181518/22267106658 links Modern Fronts and Wildwood Cabinets. #308 HubSpot company 2551073029 (Wildwood Cabinets, Inc.); #457 HubSpot company 37315206430 (Modern Fronts DBA - Wildwood Cabinets Incorporated). Account naming + payment-timeline transition consistent with a 2022 rebrand.

User-confirmed churn (2026-06-15). Evidence: HubSpot Instance Sync Sheet pay_status=Cancelled. No MRR since Jan 2026. Last payment $778/mo on 2026-01-13.
adjustments.evidence: HubSpot Instance Sync Sheet pay_status=Cancelled. No MRR since Jan 2026. Last payment $778/mo on 2026-01-13.

adjustments.reason: 107 customer-name → allmoxy_customer_id mappings for Stripe Connect revenue attribution. Source: Stripe Connect Revenue sheets carry the customer NAME on each transaction; this file resolves names to canonical Allmoxy IDs so per-customer Connect rollups join correctly. Roughly 0 names remain unmapped (acceptable — typically dormant or one-off accounts).

88 customer record(s) auto-classified status='never_paid' and excluded_from_logo_count=true (lifetime ≤ $0). Split: 60 pure never-paid (0 transactions) + 28 zero-net-revenue-with-activity (had transactions but they netted to $0 — refunds, chargebacks, reversed billing). Rationale: net economic contribution to Allmoxy was zero either way, so they should not appear in churn or logo counts. Sample: #333 Quality Kitchens Inc., #285 Mitchell Wood Worx, #44 Archer Mills, ….
